meta.author: Aleksandar Spasevski
meta.doc_subtitle: Less organic traffic, but a higher share of it converts as informational queries move to AI Overviews — with buyer demand strengthening across the brand's channels. Modernize the existing foundation via site-level template upgrades + per-URL refreshes, restore index hygiene, close the 2.49M-SV store-gap surface.

mini_reports.name: Spot & Tango content pattern — 20 pair audit
mini_reports.summary: Full evidence trail for the S&T structural pattern claim, verified across 20 head-to-head article pairs.

mini_reports.name: Top 50 BLOG losers + Ideal Sitemap mapping
mini_reports.summary: Per-URL bleed inventory, each joined against the Ideal Sitemap action recommendations.

mini_reports.name: Site-level + template upgrades
mini_reports.summary: Template-level changes that lift every page at once — visible Updated date, topical-cluster internal-link widget, blog→PDP link surface, schema.

mini_reports.name: Store Gap — why, how, where (deep dive)
mini_reports.summary: The 2.49M-SV opportunity unpacked: the 5 compounding causes, the 5-step detection pipeline, and the per-PDP location map.

kpis.label: Foundation signals held
kpis.detail: All audited Bernie's URLs carry vet credentials + last-updated dates. The pre-2025 fundamentals are in place — the market moved underneath the content.

kpis.label: Organic CVR Q1 YoY
kpis.detail: 0.67% → 2.17%, a 3.2× lift. As AI Overviews absorb the informational symptom queries, the organic visitors who still click through are far more commercial — so the store converts a much higher share of remaining organic traffic, in step with stronger buyer demand across the brand's channels.

kpis.label: Organic transactions Q1 YoY
kpis.detail: 186 → 286 transactions despite −52% sessions — absolute organic purchase volume grew year-over-year, part of broader purchase growth across the brand's channels.

kpis.label: GSC clicks YoY 90d
kpis.detail: 16,411 → 7,800. Half of last year's organic clicks gone; bleed concentrated Aug 2025 – Apr 2026 (Aug 2025 core update + AIO saturation).

kpis.label: Crawled — not indexed
kpis.detail: +1,178 (+79%) since Feb 21. Param pollution + duplicate URLs + pagination + thin taxonomy. Even perfect content refreshes won't help if Google won't index the canonical URL.

kpis.label: Store in-SKU opportunity
kpis.detail: Monthly SV across in-SKU kws Bernie's store doesn't rank for. Only 3 PDPs rank ≤20 anywhere today. 82% of the surface lives at the PDP layer. Full why/how/where: store-gap.html.

production_cadence.headline: Current cadence: 12 article upgrades + 10 new blogs per month

proposed.rationale: Most upside is content hygiene + cornerstone upgrades, not net-new articles. Shift production weight from net-new toward refresh-with-S&T-pattern.

production_cadence.implication: At current cadence (12 upgrades/mo), all top 50 BLOG losers ship by end of M3 (4 months) — Q3 plan completes within capacity. At proposed cadence (20/mo), top 50 ships in M2 + 4 PDP refreshes + 2 site-level template changes in M1. Either way, capacity is sufficient.
constraints: Each page upgrade is a hands-on senior-SEO content pass with editorial review

site_level_upgrades.headline: Template-level fixes — one change each, lifts every blog post sitewide
site_level_upgrades.summary: Per-URL refreshes carry a 20–22 day cycle each. Template-level changes are one-shot edits to the Divi Theme Builder that propagate to every existing and future blog post. Identified from the 20-pair S&T structural audit (st-pattern.html). Each item below = single template edit, sitewide lift.

items.name: Visible 'Last Updated' date in blog template
items.current: Bernie's already updates content (dateModified ships in BlogPosting schema) but the freshness signal is NOT visible to readers or to Google's heading-outline reader. S&T shows both publish + modified date prominently.
items.change: Surface dateModified visibly in the byline area (e.g. 'Updated May 15, 2026' next to 'By: Bernie's'). Add og:modified_time meta tag.
items.why_it_matters: Visible freshness signal is a measured E-E-A-T factor. Bernie's already has the data — currently invisible to users + AIO source-selection.
items.lift: Sitewide (250+ blog posts). One change to Divi template + meta tag.

items.name: Topical-cluster-aware 'Featured Blogs' widget (custom blog linking per category)
items.scope: Footer sidebar of every blog post
items.current: Static 'Featured Blogs' widget shows the same fixed editorial picks on every post. Articles in the Perfect Poop cluster currently link to unrelated articles instead of other Perfect Poop cluster content.
items.change: Replace static widget with topical-cluster-aware widget that pulls 4-6 related articles from the same cluster (Perfect Poop / Healthy Hips / OMG Omegas / Charming Chompers / Marvelous Mobility). Driven by the same cluster_label that the Ideal Sitemap workflow already assigns.
items.why_it_matters: Strengthens topical clusters Google reads as authority signals. Reduces hop-count from related cluster article → main_keyword target. Bernie's URL path is already 3 segments (/blogs/bernies-blog/<slug>) — every related-content link tightens cluster cohesion.
items.lift: Sitewide. Each cluster gets ~30-60 articles cross-linked instead of 0-2 today.

items.name: Increase blog→blog internal links (in-context, cluster-aware)
items.scope: Every blog post · 3-5 new contextual links per article
items.current: Audit data shows avg 4-5 internal links per blog post; many are weak (footer-template links to /university hubs that themselves aren't indexed). Cluster-internal cross-link density is near zero.
items.change: Per refresh, add 3-5 in-body contextual links to other articles in the same topical cluster. Mid-paragraph anchor links with descriptive anchor text (not 'click here'). Standardize on cluster head terms.
items.why_it_matters: Concentrates PageRank within cluster (vs leaking to thin-taxonomy hubs). Helps Google understand the article isn't an isolated piece but part of a coherent topical surface — exactly the signal AIO uses for source authority.
items.lift: +150-250 cluster-internal links sitewide once top 50 are refreshed. Multiplier on AIO citation surface.
items.effort: M (per refresh, applied during top-50 cycle)

items.name: Increase blog→PDP internal links (commercial funnel)
items.current: Existing blog content ranks for some category terms (digestive, fiber, probiotic) but the authority signal doesn't funnel to the corresponding store PDP. 15 WRONG_URL keywords confirmed where a non-store URL ranks ≤30 but the PDP doesn't.
items.change: Per refresh: add 1-2 contextual blog→PDP links in the body (mid-article, in a recommendation paragraph), plus a sidebar Related Products card. Cluster-aware: Perfect Poop cluster articles link to /products/bernies-perfect-poop; Healthy Hips cluster to /products/healthy-hips; etc.
items.why_it_matters: Closes the subdomain split (www.bernies.com blog ↔ store.bernies.com PDP). Directly addresses 15 WRONG_URL keywords. Builds canonical signal toward the commercial page Google should be ranking for category terms.
items.lift: +50-100 blog→PDP links sitewide after the top-50 refresh cycle. Funnels existing blog authority into store-gap capture.

items.name: Demote footer H3s to non-heading elements (eliminate ~1,250 pollution H3s)
items.current: Every Bernie's blog post ships 4-5 footer H3s as empty heading shells: About the Author, Keep Learning at Bernie's University, Featured Blogs, Bernie's Perfect Poop (or other SKU), and the kicker — $13.99 - $46.99 (the product price displayed as an H3 on 19 of 20 audited articles).
items.change: Single Divi Theme Builder change: demote these to non-heading elements (styled <div> or <p>). Eliminates ~5 EMPTY_SECTION H3s per article × 250+ blog posts = ~1,250 pollution H3s removed.
items.why_it_matters: Google's heading-outline reader currently sees a 'Pricing' topical section in every article, which is false. Bernie's has 81 EMPTY_SECTION + 22 THIN_H2 instances in the 20-article audit cohort — most are these footer-template H3s.

items.name: FAQPage schema on articles with FAQ sections
items.scope: Every blog post with an existing FAQ Q&A section
items.current: Bernie's has more FAQ Q&A pairs than S&T in some articles (probiotics, acid-reflux) but ships no FAQPage schema markup.
items.change: Conditional template logic: if article body contains an FAQ section, auto-emit FAQPage schema with the Q&A pairs. Standard JSON-LD format.
items.why_it_matters: Direct AIO/SERP enhancement. Bernie's already wrote the FAQs; the schema is the missing layer to surface them.
items.lift: Estimated 40-60 articles eligible immediately. Free citation/snippet surface.

items.name: Auto-emit BreadcrumbList + Article schema improvements
items.current: BlogPosting schema present on most articles; Breadcrumb data not surfaced in JSON-LD; dateModified in schema but not visible.
items.change: Template-level: emit BreadcrumbList JSON-LD on every article (Home > Blog > Cluster > Article). Ensure dateModified in JSON-LD matches the visible 'Updated' date from TPL-01.
items.why_it_matters: Breadcrumb schema = SERP breadcrumb display (visual differentiator). Schema-visible-data parity = trust signal.

items.name: Author/vet credentials visibility — byline + body-citation pattern
items.current: Bernie's HAS vet credentials on 10/10 audited URLs (vs 58% of competitors) but they aren't surfaced consistently. AnimalBiome pattern (verified competitor): 'Reviewed by Dr. X, DVM' header note + body-cited vet attribution. Bernie's already has the credibility — currently invisible.
items.change: Template byline: 'By: Bernie's · Reviewed by Dr. X, DVM · Updated [date]'. Per-refresh: when an article makes a clinical claim, body-cite the reviewing vet inline. Add the byline + bio + inline vet citations.
items.why_it_matters: Bernie's strongest moat vs Spot & Tango (S&T top-gainers deliberately omit DVM citation). Surfacing what S&T omits = direct E-E-A-T differentiation on YMYL queries.

items.name: Internal linking from blog→Knowledge Base + Knowledge Base→Blog/PDP
items.current: Knowledge Base + University sections are a key brand asset but currently siloed. Articles within KB rarely link to corresponding blog cornerstone or PDP.
items.change: Add bidirectional linking: each KB article gets a 'Learn more in our blog' contextual link to the cluster main article. Each cluster main blog gets a 'Quick answers in our Knowledge Base' inset linking to relevant KB entries. Same cluster_label keying.
items.why_it_matters: Concentrates the existing KB authority into the topical clusters that need lifting. Doesn't remove or restructure the KB; just adds cross-link density.

items.name: URL-path consolidation (long-term)
items.current: Bernie's blog URLs are 3 segments (/blogs/bernies-blog/<slug>); S&T's are 2 (/blog/<slug>). Click-depth from home is 3-4 hops vs S&T's 2.
items.change: Long-term migration to 2-segment paths. Lowers crawl depth, improves PageRank distribution. Combined with redirect-chain cleanup discovered in pairs 15+16 (S&T audit).
items.why_it_matters: Defer to M3+ — large surface; phase the 250+ URL 301-redirect plan carefully.

trend.headline: Less traffic, more buyers — organic Q1 YoY: sessions ▼-52%, transactions ▲+54%, CVR ▲+223%
trend.annotation_text: Q1 2025 → Q1 2026: Organic sessions 27,617 → 13,160 (−52%); Organic transactions 186 → 286 (+54%); Organic CVR 0.67% → 2.17% (+223%). As AI Overviews absorb informational queries, the organic visitors who remain convert far more — and buyer demand is strengthening across the brand's channels in parallel. May 2026 is partial (through May 26).

quarterly_chart.headline: Bernie's Best — Organic Search · quarterly: traffic fell sharply, but a higher share of the remaining clicks converted
quarterly_chart.subtitle: Q1 2025 → Q1 2026. Q2 2026 (est) shown hatched/dashed — Apr 2026 actual + May run-rate projection + June projected from May.
quarterly_chart.estimate_note: Q2 2026 (est) = Apr 2026 (3,728 sess / 87 tx) + projected May (4,217 / 86 from May 1-21 run-rate) + projected June (using May run-rate as proxy). Sessions trend continues down; CVR holds the new ~2.1% plateau.

quarterly_yoy_infographic.headline: Q1 2025 vs Q1 2026 — the headline inversion in one frame
quarterly_yoy_infographic.subtitle: Organic only. Fewer visitors, more buyers, a higher share converting.

panels.context: Aug 2025 core update + AI Overview saturation collapsed CTR on YMYL queries.
Despite the traffic loss, absolute purchase volume from organic GREW year-over-year.
As AI Overviews absorb informational queries, the organic visitors who remain are far more likely to buy — the same buyer-demand strength showing across the brand's channels.

ahrefs_trajectory.headline: Two cliffs: Aug-Oct 2025 + May 2026
ahrefs_trajectory.annotation: Aug 2024 = peak traffic month (9,273). Jul 2025 = peak pages-ranking month (393). Two distinct cliffs: Aug-Oct 2025 (Aug 2025 core update + AIO maturation: −48% in 60 days) and May 2026 (March 2026 core update trailing edge: pages 188 → 130 in 30 days).

index_health.headline: 2,670 pages crawled but not indexed — +79% in 84 days
index_health.headline_text: Sample of 1,000 URLs shows the categories: URL parameter pollution, duplicate URL paths, review pagination, blog pagination loops, thin taxonomy hubs.

categories.name: Blog posts (real content)
categories.example: Real articles Google chose not to index

aio_saturation.headline: 89% of Bernie's top kws now carry an AI Overview
aio_saturation.ctr_note: Industry CTR studies show AIO reduces #1 organic CTR by ~40–70%; #5–#20 CTR by ~60–90%. Bernie's rankings mostly held; clicks per ranking position collapsed.

store_gap_summary.headline: 3,338 in-SKU kws / 2.49M monthly SV / effectively zero capture
why_short: Topical alignment gap — PDPs barely use category terms in indexable structure
Schema gap — no Product/Review/FAQ/Organization JSON-LD on PDPs
Subdomain split (store.bernies.com vs www.bernies.com) + weak blog→PDP cross-link
Parameter pollution diluting canonical PDP signal (Crawled — not indexed pile)
5×5 cannibalization — Learn More pages on www outrank store PDPs
store_gap_summary.how_short: 5-step detection: build the in-SKU keyword universe → pull ranked keywords for both subdomains (blog + store) → classify (PURE_OPPORTUNITY / WRONG_URL / STORE_GOOD) → dedup cross-SKU overlap → manual SERP-intent validation on the top 60 keywords.
store_gap_summary.where_short: 5 PDPs on store.bernies.com — Perfect Poop (45 rankings, mostly branded) · Healthy Hips (1) · OMG Omegas (2-3) · Charming Chompers (2-3) · Marvelous Mobility (0). Plus 5 'Learn More' pages on www.bernies.com that cannibalize the PDPs.

head_kws: hip and joint supplement for dogs

st_pattern_verified.headline: S&T content pattern — verified across 20 head-to-head article pairs
st_pattern_verified.method_summary: 40 articles analyzed (20 S&T winners + 20 Bernie's matched losers on the same query). Parsed each into structured sections, measured heading hierarchy, semantic patterns, and content-gap inventory.

headline_findings.label: S&T avg H3 / article
headline_findings.note: Bernie's averages 36% of S&T's H3 count

headline_findings.label: Numbered H3 enumeration (total across 20)
headline_findings.note: 55× delta — the single biggest measured signal

headline_findings.label: Avg body length
headline_findings.note: S&T is 2.44× longer on average. In every single pair, S&T is longer.

headline_findings.label: Articles with ≥20 H3
headline_findings.note: S&T runs deep H3 enumeration as a pattern, not a one-off

headline_findings.label: EMPTY_SECTION + THIN_H2 (gaps)
headline_findings.note: Most of Bernie's 103 are footer-template H3 pollution

pattern_elements: Word count 2,100-3,500 (most Bernie's articles at ~1,500-1,700 — need depth, not trimming)
Heading depth: 6-10 H2 + 15-25 H3 per article (Bernie's avg 8.2 H3 today; target 18)
Numbered H3 enumeration for every parallel list (causes/symptoms/treatments/prevention)
30-60w intro paragraph before any bullet list (S&T does this; Bernie's BARE_LIST count is already low)
Brand byline + visible publish date + visible 'Updated' date (TPL-01)
FAQ section as 2nd-to-last H2 (3-5 Q&A) — recommended not mandatory
'Final Thoughts' conclusion (the most consistent gainer element)
5-8 inline product mentions + sidebar Related Products (down from Bernie's current ~36/article)
st_pattern_verified.bernies_overlay: Bernie's wins what S&T deliberately omits: 'Reviewed by Dr. X, DVM' header note (AnimalBiome-style), body-cited vet credentials on clinical claims, citations to Cornell/NIH/Merck on YMYL claims. Apply S&T structural skeleton + AnimalBiome E-E-A-T overlay.

p0_articles.fix_note: Largest single-article gap. Promote 7 inline causes to numbered H3s + add Vet Diagnosis + Treatment + Prevention H2s.
YMYL emergency topic. Expand to 3,000w+. Add numbered H3 cause categories + dedicated Treatment section.
Bernie's H2 structure is already right; just needs body depth under each H2. Fastest restructure of the P0 set.
Bernie's runs 14 H3 (above its avg) — body expansion is the lever, not restructure.

top50_losers_summary.headline: Top 50 BLOG losers — full inventory connected to Ideal Sitemap actions
top50_losers_summary.narrative: The per-URL bleed inventory spans 50 URLs, each joined against the Ideal Sitemap workflow's per-cluster action recommendations. 15 of 50 already mapped to Perfect Poop cluster actions; remaining 35 await the next-4-SKU cluster runs.
top50_losers_summary.ideal_sitemap_connection: The Ideal Sitemap workflow is HQDM's reusable workflow that maps a client's current content state vs. ideal state per topical cluster. Output keyed by main_keyword with rule-based suggested_actions: upgrade_main_article / solve_cannibalization / manage_redirects / consolidate_learn_more / write_new_article / review_manually. Bernie's MVP cluster (Perfect Poop, 39 main_keyword rows) drives the top-50 mapping. Same workflow reusable for Bernie's 4 remaining SKU clusters and every future HQDM client.

top_5_visible_in_dashboard.target_kw: my dog has diarrhea at night but is acting fine
dog food for inflammatory bowel disease
how long after dog eats do they poop

top_winners_q1yoy.headline: Page-level Q1 2025 → Q1 2026 — the conversion winners
top_winners_q1yoy.read: PDPs do the conversion work; blog drives volume. /products/bernies-perfect-poop adds +45 transactions despite −18% sessions (CVR 3.60% → 6.36%). /products/charming-chompers gains BOTH +69% sessions AND +340% transactions. Scale the Charming Chompers / Marvelous Mobility pattern to the other 2 PDPs (Healthy Hips, OMG Omegas) — directly validates Lever 2.

q3_kpi_targets.kpi: Store PDP rankings ≤20 on in-SKU kws

positives: Foundation signals held — vet credentials + last-updated dates on 10/10 audited URLs
GSC 90d PoP +6.3% — bleed has stopped at sitewide level (Q1 2026 recovery)
Organic transactions Q1'25 → Q1'26: 186 → 286 (+54%) despite −52% sessions — buyers consolidate
Organic CVR Q1'25 → Q1'26: 0.67% → 2.17% (+223%) — 3.2× lift in purchase rate as informational queries move to AI Overviews and the remaining organic clicks skew commercial
Direct sessions flat (10,742 → 10,372, −3%) — and purchases via Direct + Email grew alongside organic Q1 YoY (no brand-pull erosion)
S&T content pattern verified across 20 head-to-head pairs (st-pattern.html) — recipe is measured, not opinion
/products/charming-chompers gained BOTH +69% sessions AND +340% transactions in Q1'26
Verified Lever 2 thesis: PDPs do the conversion work, blog drives volume
Production cadence sufficient: 12 upgrades/mo current ships top-50 by M3 within capacity
negatives: GSC clicks YoY 90d: 16,411 → 7,800 (−52.5%) — half of last year's clicks gone
Crawled-not-indexed: 1,492 → 2,670 in 84 days (+79%) — index health actively degrading
AI Overview presence: ~20-40% (May 2025) → 89% (May 2026) on Bernie's top kws
Store ranks 59 kws total; 75% are branded; only ~6 truly generic / 230 SV/month
Marvelous Mobility ranks for 0 keywords; 4 of 5 PDPs effectively absent from category SERPs
Bernie's averages 36% of S&T's H3 count (8.2 vs 22.6); uses 4 numbered-enum headings vs S&T's 219
Footer-template H3 pollution: ~1,250 EMPTY_SECTION H3s sitewide ($13.99 - $46.99 as an H3 on 19 of 20 audited articles)
Spot & Tango grew +2,078% top-10 visibility while Bernie's lost 50% — pattern-driven reshuffle

levers.name: Top-50 BLOG losers — sequenced refresh (S&T pattern)
levers.why: Top 50 carries 75% of all blog YoY loss. Each URL joined against Ideal Sitemap actions (upgrade / cannibalization / redirect / consolidate). At current 12-upgrades/mo cadence, the full 50 ships by M3. At proposed 20/mo cadence, ships by M2. See top-50-losers.html for the full inventory + action mapping.

actions.name: Refresh dog-has-diarrhea-at-night (1,446 clicks lost; manage_redirects + solve_cannibalization)
Refresh help-why-is-my-dog-throwing-up-undigested-food (983 lost; mirror Pet Honesty pattern)
Pivot enzymatic-toothpaste-for-dogs to comparative listicle format (683 lost)
Upgrade what-to-feed-a-dog-with-inflammatory-bowel-disease → funnel to Perfect Poop PDP (upgrade_main_article)
Refresh its-potty-time (#4 → target #2; consolidate_learn_more + manage_redirects + solve_cannibalization + upgrade)
Refresh signs-my-dog-needs-fiber (#21 → top-10; upgrade_main_article)
Refresh black-dog-poop-causes-treatments (#27 → #15)
Refresh how-do-i-make-my-dogs-poop-smell-better (#16 → #8; full sitemap action stack)
Refresh help-my-dog-is-throwing-up-white-foam (303 ranked kws, biggest surface)
Refresh help-my-dog-is-throwing-up-clear-liquid (#14 → #8)
Next 20 from top-50-losers.html (M2 batch — 20 articles in M2)
Remaining 20 from top-50-losers.html (M3 close-out batch)

levers.outcome: Reclaim 30-50% of YoY click loss on top 50 = ~+2,800-4,800 monthly clicks. All refreshes apply the S&T pattern (st-pattern.html) PLUS template-level upgrades (TPL-01 through TPL-08).

levers.name: Store PDP build-out (5 SKUs)
levers.why: 3,338 in-SKU kws / 2.49M monthly SV with near-zero capture today (store-gap.html). PDPs need topical content aligned with category kws + Product/Review/FAQ/Organization schema + internal linking from existing blog content (TPL-04). The Charming Chompers + Marvelous Mobility Q1 wins prove the pattern.

actions.name: Refresh Perfect Poop PDP — 1.38M SV addressable; H2s = category kws + Product/Review/FAQ schema
Refresh Healthy Hips PDP — 444K SV; currently 0 store rankings
Sitewide Organization + BreadcrumbList template (prerequisite — TPL-07)
Product+Review+FAQ schema on Perfect Poop PDP
Product+Review+FAQ schema on Healthy Hips PDP
Refresh OMG Omegas PDP — 426K SV
Refresh Charming Chompers PDP — 244K SV; 'alternative to Greenies' positioning
Schema on OMG Omegas PDP
Schema on Charming Chompers PDP
Refresh Marvelous Mobility PDP — composite with Hips
Schema on Marvelous Mobility PDP
15 WRONG_URL fixes — add PDP links from ranking blog URLs (TPL-04)

levers.outcome: Capturing even 1% of 2.49M monthly SV = 25K monthly visits = 10× the store's organic traffic. The Charming Chompers Q1 pattern (+69% sessions, +340% tx) shows the upside is real.

levers.name: Site-level / template upgrades (NEW — sitewide lifts)
levers.why: 10 template-level changes (TPL-01 through TPL-10) — each a one-shot Divi Theme Builder edit that propagates to every existing and future blog post. Identified from the 20-pair S&T structural audit. See site-level-upgrades.html for the full spec.

actions.name: Visible 'Last Updated' date in blog template (+ og:modified_time)
Topical-cluster-aware Featured Blogs widget (replaces static)
Demote footer H3s to non-heading elements (~1,250 pollution H3s removed)
FAQPage schema on articles with existing FAQ Q&A sections
Author/vet credentials visibility (byline + body-citation pattern)
Increase blog→blog internal links (cluster-aware, per refresh)
Increase blog→PDP internal links (commercial funnel, per refresh)
BreadcrumbList JSON-LD + Article schema improvements
Blog ↔ Knowledge Base cross-linking
URL-path consolidation (M3+)

levers.outcome: Sitewide. TPL-05 alone removes ~1,250 pollution H3s. TPL-01 + TPL-08 surface E-E-A-T signals Bernie's already has but currently hides. TPL-02 + TPL-03 + TPL-04 build the topical-cluster + commercial-funnel link surface.

levers.name: Index hygiene — restore the not-indexed pile
levers.why: 2,670 pages crawled-not-indexed (+1,178 in 84 days). Root causes: URL parameter pollution, duplicate URL paths (root vs /blogs/bernies-blog/), review pagination, blog pagination, thin taxonomy hubs.

actions.name: Deploy canonical tags for URL parameter pollution (?_gl, ?utm, ?_pos, ?_sid, ?reviews-page, ?srsltid)
301 redirect duplicate URL paths (root-level → /blogs/bernies-blog/<slug>/)
Weekly GSC Coverage report monitoring + alert if +100 in a week
Review-pagination URLs → rel=canonical to /perfect-poop/reviews/
Blog-pagination loops → rel=canonical to clean URL
Thin-taxonomy audit (/university/<dim>/<value>/) — KEEP / CONSOLIDATE / NOINDEX decisions

levers.outcome: Restoring index health on 30% of the 2,670 not-indexed pages = ~800 pages back in Google's index. Found revenue without writing new content.

levers.name: Re-target wrong-intent URLs + new blog production
levers.why: Wrong-intent: 2 URLs competing in SERPs whose audience doesn't match Bernie's customer. New blog production: at proposed 5 new/mo cadence (down from 10), topic queue is per-SKU PURE_OPPORTUNITY kws. Each new blog applies S&T pattern + TPL-* upgrades.

actions.name: /perfect-poop/dog-stool-odor → re-target to 'why does my dog's poop smell so bad' (SV 2,900)
/university/dig-deeper/boswellia-frankincense → re-target OR sunset
5 new blog posts (M2 batch) — S&T pattern + TPL-* checklist
5 new blog posts (M3 batch)

levers.outcome: Small click recovery from wrong-intent; new blog cadence rebuilds long-tail at higher-quality structural baseline. Compounds with refresh velocity.

levers.name: Off-page program — PR stunt + citations + affiliate
levers.why: S&T's April 2026 PR stunt generated 111 real-auth backlinks (DR avg 66, traffic 7.4M) — biggest single-event ROI in 5-peer cohort. Bernie's missing 4 of 8 standard business citations all peers have. Affiliate network presence (DR 60-74) provides sustained brand-coupon SERP surface.

actions.name: PR stunt — quirky-job-posting via Businesswire, tied to new SKU launch ($350-$2K)
Claim BBB.org + Glassdoor + RocketReach + LeadIQ + Owler profiles (5 missing citations)
Activate affiliate program (Refersion/Impact/ShareASale) + submit to coupon networks
Monitor HARO/Qwoted/Featured/Connectively daily for pet roundup source calls
Backlink audit + identify referring-domain opportunities
Tier 1 PR outreach batch — 10-15 pitches to DR 70+ publishers
Pet-industry publication outreach — 15-20 pitches
Brand mention reclamation — convert unlinked mentions to links
Tier 3 niche outreach + M1/M2 follow-ups
Q3 off-page close-out review

levers.outcome: 30+ high-DR placements from PR stunt in 30 days. 5 new real-auth refdomains (DR 71-93) from citation claims. 5-7 new affiliate RDs (DR 60-74). 2-3 HARO/Qwoted placements/quarter at DR 85+.

snapshot.eyebrow: HQDM Search Intelligence · Prepared by Aleksandar Spasevski
snapshot.title: Bernie's Best
snapshot.subtitle: Q3 2026 · DTC dog supplements · Client Snapshot
snapshot.footer: Client snapshot · Q3 2026 · Prepared by Aleksandar Spasevski
snapshot.exec_html: Organic traffic is down hard — roughly half of last year's clicks are gone (−52% YoY) as Google's AI Overviews absorb the informational searches that used to bring visitors. But the people who do arrive are buying far more often: your organic conversion rate more than tripled (0.67% → 2.17%, +223%) and organic transactions actually rose +54% year over year despite the traffic drop. The competitive story is Spot & Tango, whose top-10 keyword footprint exploded +2,078% off a verified PR-stunt link pattern — the highest-ROI tactic to replicate. The plan refreshes the top blog losers on that pattern, builds out the product pages, fixes index hygiene, and runs a PR push.

line.title: Organic Performance · Monthly (traffic + transactions)
line.caption: Organic visitors (left axis) and e-commerce transactions (right axis). A dashed tail marks the partial current month.

snapshot.boxes_reading_html: Fewer visitors, but they convert far better — transactions and conversion rate are both up sharply year over year even as AI Overviews absorb informational traffic. The current quarter is partial.

focus.title: Refresh top blog losers
focus.body: Sequenced refresh of the top-50 blog pages that lost ground, using the Spot & Tango pattern that’s working in the category.

focus.title: Build out the product pages
focus.body: Expand the five SKU product pages so commercial searches land on pages built to convert.

focus.title: Index hygiene
focus.body: Restore the 2,670 crawled-but-not-indexed URLs by cleaning up parameter pollution and duplicate pages.

focus.title: PR stunt + citations
focus.body: Run the PR-stunt + citation + affiliate push that drove Spot & Tango’s footprint gain.

competitors.title: Competitor Movement · Organic (top-10 keywords)
competitors.note_html: Spot & Tango (★) is the story — a +2,078% jump in top-10 keywords off a verified PR-stunt backlink pattern. That's the highest-ROI tactic to replicate. ★ marks the verified pattern.
